content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : File - 87207(b)                                Date : April 17, 1979


                                                    /71-79-154

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Lee Rosenthal

Subject:

       At the opinion request meeting of April 16, 1979, we
decided that under Section 87207(b)(3) the gross receipts
of a bank do not include repayments of loans. Hence,
if an official owned 20% of the stock in a bank, he would
disclose a debtor of the bank only if that debtor paid
$50,000 in interest during the year (20% of $50,000 =
$10,000 - the disclosure threshold). If the debtor, in
addition to paying interest on the loan, had also repaid
the loan, the repayment is not included in determining
whether the $10,000 gross receipts threshold has been
reached.

LR:plh

sections.conclusion: Under Section 87207(b)(3) the gross receipts of a bank do not include repayments of loans. Hence, if an official owned 20% of the stock in a bank, he would disclose a debtor of the bank only if that debtor paid $50,000 in interest during the year (20% of $50,000 = $10,000 - the disclosure threshold). If the debtor, in addition to paying interest on the loan, had also repaid the loan, the repayment is not included in determining whether the $10,000 gross receipts threshold has been reached.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether loan repayments should be included in calculating a bank's gross receipts for purposes of determining disclosure thresholds under Section 87207(b)(3) when a public official owns stock in the bank.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Loan repayments are not included in calculating a bank's gross receipts for disclosure threshold purposes under Section 87207(b)(3). A public official with 20% ownership would only disclose bank debtors if they paid $50,000 in interest (resulting in $10,000 threshold to the official's interest), and loan principal repayments do not count toward this threshold.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal question or conclusion sections present; extracted Q/C content from body text. Document is brief advisory response from April 1979 opinion request meeting. Initialed by LR (Lee Rosenthal) with plh typist initials.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether loan repayments should be included in calculating a bank's gross receipts for purposes of determining disclosure thresholds under Section 87207(b)(3) when a public official owns stock in the bank.

CONCLUSION: Under Section 87207(b)(3) the gross receipts of a bank do not include repayments of loans. Hence, if an official owned 20% of the stock in a bank, he would disclose a debtor of the bank only if that debtor paid $50,000 in interest during the year (20% of $50,000 = $10,000 - the disclosure threshold). If the debtor, in addition to paying interest on the loan, had also repaid the loan, the repayment is not included in determining whether the $10,000 gross receipts threshold has been reached.

Hence, if an official owned 20% of the stock in a bank, he would disclose a debtor of the bank only if that debtor paid $50,000 in interest during the year (20% of $50,000 = $10,000 - the disclosure threshold). If the debtor, in addition to paying interest on the loan, had also repaid the loan, the repayment is not included in determining whether the $10,000 gross receipts threshold has been reached. LR:plh
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that loan repayments should not be included in calculating gross receipts for a bank's disclosure threshold purposes under Section 87207(b)(3). The analysis demonstrates application of this rule to a hypothetical scenario where a public official owns 20% of a bank.
